name: sigstore-protobuf-specs

metadata.name: sigstore-protobuf-specs
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A library for serializing and deserializing Sigstore messages

documentation:
sigstore-protobuf-specs
=======================

These are the Python language bindings for Sigstore's protobuf specs.

See the [repository's README](https://github.com/sigstore/protobuf-specs)
for more information.

modules.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.io.intoto
documentation: Generated by the protocol buffer compiler.  DO NOT EDIT!
sources: envelope.proto
plugin: python-betterproto
This file has been @generated

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.io.intoto.Envelope

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.io.intoto.Envelope.payload
documentation: Message to be signed. (In JSON, this is encoded as base64.) REQUIRED.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.io.intoto.Envelope.payload_type
documentation: String unambiguously identifying how to interpret payload. REQUIRED.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.io.intoto.Envelope.signatures
documentation:
Signature over:     PAE(type, payload) Where PAE is defined as: PAE(type,
payload) = "DSSEv1" + SP + LEN(type) + SP + type + SP + LEN(payload) + SP +
payload +               = concatenation SP              = ASCII space
[0x20] "DSSEv1"        = ASCII [0x44, 0x53, 0x53, 0x45, 0x76, 0x31] LEN(s)
= ASCII decimal encoding of the byte length of s, with no leading zeros
REQUIRED (length >= 1).
An authenticated message of arbitrary type.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.io.intoto.Signature.sig
documentation: Signature itself. (In JSON, this is encoded as base64.) REQUIRED.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.io.intoto.Signature.keyid
documentation: *Unauthenticated* hint identifying which public key was used. OPTIONAL.

modules.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1
documentation: Generated by the protocol buffer compiler.  DO NOT EDIT!
sources: sigstore_rekor.proto
plugin: python-betterproto
This file has been @generated

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.KindVersion

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.KindVersion.kind
documentation:
Kind is the type of entry being stored in the log. See here for a list:
https://github.com/sigstore/rekor/tree/main/pkg/types

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.KindVersion.version
documentation: The specific api version of the type.
KindVersion contains the entry's kind and api version.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.Checkpoint
documentation:
The checkpoint MUST contain an origin string as a unique log identifier,
the tree size, and the root hash. It MAY also be followed by optional data,
and clients MUST NOT assume optional data. The checkpoint MUST also contain
a signature over the root hash (tree head). The checkpoint MAY contain
additional signatures, but the first SHOULD be the signature from the log.
Checkpoint contents are concatenated with newlines into a single string.
The checkpoint format is described in https://github.com/transparency-
dev/formats/blob/main/log/README.md and
https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/tlog-checkpoint.md. An example
implementation can be found in
https://github.com/sigstore/rekor/blob/main/pkg/util/signed_note.go

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.InclusionProof

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.InclusionProof.log_index
documentation: The index of the entry in the tree it was written to.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.InclusionProof.root_hash
documentation:
The hash digest stored at the root of the merkle tree at the time the proof
was generated.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.InclusionProof.tree_size
documentation: The size of the merkle tree at the time the proof was generated.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.InclusionProof.hashes
documentation:
A list of hashes required to compute the inclusion proof, sorted in order
from leaf to root. Note that leaf and root hashes are not included. The
root hash is available separately in this message, and the leaf hash should
be calculated by the client.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.InclusionProof.checkpoint
documentation:
Signature of the tree head, as of the time of this proof was generated. See
above info on 'Checkpoint' for more details.

InclusionProof is the proof returned from the transparency log. Can be used
for offline or online verification against the log.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.InclusionPromise
documentation:
The inclusion promise is calculated by Rekor. It's calculated as a
signature over a canonical JSON serialization of the persisted entry, the
log ID, log index and the integration timestamp. See https://github.com/sig
store/rekor/blob/a6e58f72b6b18cc06cefe61808efd562b9726330/pkg/api/entries.g
o#L54 The format of the signature depends on the transparency log's public
key. If the signature algorithm requires a hash function and/or a signature
scheme (e.g. RSA) those has to be retrieved out-of-band from the log's
operators, together with the public key. This is used to verify the
integration timestamp's value and that the log has promised to include the
entry.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.TransparencyLogEntry

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.TransparencyLogEntry.log_index
documentation: The global index of the entry, used when querying the log by index.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.TransparencyLogEntry.log_id
documentation: The unique identifier of the log.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.TransparencyLogEntry.kind_version
documentation:
The kind (type) and version of the object associated with this entry. These
values are required to construct the entry during verification.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.TransparencyLogEntry.integrated_time
documentation: The UNIX timestamp from the log when the entry was persisted.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.TransparencyLogEntry.inclusion_promise
documentation:
The inclusion promise/signed entry timestamp from the log. Required for
v0.1 bundles, and MUST be verified. Optional for >= v0.2 bundles, and
SHOULD be verified when present. Also may be used as a signed timestamp.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.TransparencyLogEntry.inclusion_proof
documentation:
The inclusion proof can be used for offline or online verification that the
entry was appended to the log, and that the log has not been altered.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.rekor.v1.TransparencyLogEntry.canonicalized_body
documentation:
Optional. The canonicalized transparency log entry, used to reconstruct the
Signed Entry Timestamp (SET) during verification. The contents of this
field are the same as the `body` field in a Rekor response, meaning that it
does **not** include the "full" canonicalized form (of log index, ID, etc.)
which are exposed as separate fields. The verifier is responsible for
combining the `canonicalized_body`, `log_index`, `log_id`, and
`integrated_time` into the payload that the SET's signature is generated
over. This field is intended to be used in cases where the SET cannot be
produced determinisitically (e.g. inconsistent JSON field ordering,
differing whitespace, etc). If set, clients MUST verify that the signature
referenced in the `canonicalized_body` matches the signature provided in
the `Bundle.content`. If not set, clients are responsible for constructing
an equivalent payload from other sources to verify the signature.

TransparencyLogEntry captures all the details required from Rekor to
reconstruct an entry, given that the payload is provided via other means.
This type can easily be created from the existing response from Rekor.
Future iterations could rely on Rekor returning the minimal set of
attributes (excluding the payload) that are required for verifying the
inclusion promise. The inclusion promise (called SignedEntryTimestamp in
the response from Rekor) is similar to a Signed Certificate Timestamp as
described here https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962.html#section-3.2.

modules.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.bundle.v1
documentation: Generated by the protocol buffer compiler.  DO NOT EDIT!
sources: sigstore_bundle.proto
plugin: python-betterproto
This file has been @generated

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.bundle.v1.TimestampVerificationData

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.bundle.v1.TimestampVerificationData.rfc3161_timestamps
documentation:
A list of RFC3161 signed timestamps provided by the user. This can be used
when the entry has not been stored on a transparency log, or in conjunction
for a stronger trust model. Clients MUST verify the hashed message in the
message imprint against the signature in the bundle.

Various timestamped counter signatures over the artifacts signature.
Currently only RFC3161 signatures are provided. More formats may be added
in the future.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.bundle.v1.VerificationMaterial

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.bundle.v1.VerificationMaterial.tlog_entries
documentation:
An inclusion proof and an optional signed timestamp from the log. Client
verification libraries MAY provide an option to support v0.1 bundles for
backwards compatibility, which may contain an inclusion promise and not an
inclusion proof. In this case, the client MUST validate the promise.
Verifiers SHOULD NOT allow v0.1 bundles if they're used in an ecosystem
which never produced them.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.bundle.v1.VerificationMaterial.timestamp_verification_data
documentation:
Timestamp may also come from
tlog_entries.inclusion_promise.signed_entry_timestamp.

VerificationMaterial captures details on the materials used to verify
signatures. This message may be embedded in a DSSE envelope as a signature
extension. Specifically, the `ext` field of the extension will expect this
message when the signature extension is for Sigstore. This is identified by
the `kind` field in the extension, which must be set to
application/vnd.dev.sigstore.verificationmaterial;version=0.1 for Sigstore.
When used as a DSSE extension, if the `public_key` field is used to
indicate the key identifier, it MUST match the `keyid` field of the
signature the extension is attached to.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.bundle.v1.Bundle.media_type
documentation:
MUST be application/vnd.dev.sigstore.bundle.v0.3+json when when encoded as
JSON. Clients must to be able to accept media type using the previously
defined formats: * application/vnd.dev.sigstore.bundle+json;version=0.1 *
application/vnd.dev.sigstore.bundle+json;version=0.2 *
application/vnd.dev.sigstore.bundle+json;version=0.3

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.bundle.v1.Bundle.verification_material
documentation:
When a signer is identified by a X.509 certificate, a verifier MUST verify
that the signature was computed at the time the certificate was valid as
described in the Sigstore client spec: "Verification using a Bundle". <http
s://docs.google.com/document/d/1kbhK2qyPPk8SLavHzYSDM8-Ueul9_oxIMVFuWMWKz0E
/edit#heading=h.x8bduppe89ln> If the verification material contains a
public key identifier (key hint) and the `content` is a DSSE envelope, the
key hints MUST be exactly the same in the verification material and in the
DSSE envelope.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.bundle.v1.Bundle.dsse_envelope
documentation:
A DSSE envelope can contain arbitrary payloads. Verifiers must verify that
the payload type is a supported and expected type. This is part of the DSSE
protocol which is defined here: <https://github.com/secure-systems-
lab/dsse/blob/master/protocol.md> DSSE envelopes in a bundle MUST have
exactly one signture. This is a limitation from the DSSE spec, as it can
contain multiple signatures. There are two primary reasons: 1. It simplfies
the verification logic and policy 2. The bundle (currently) can only
contain a single    instance of the required verification materials During
verification a client MUST reject an envelope if the number of signatures
is not equal to one.

modules.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1
documentation: Generated by the protocol buffer compiler.  DO NOT EDIT!
sources: sigstore_trustroot.proto
plugin: python-betterproto
This file has been @generated

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TransparencyLogInstance

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TransparencyLogInstance.base_url
documentation: The base URL at which can be used to URLs for the client.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TransparencyLogInstance.hash_algorithm
documentation: The hash algorithm used for the Merkle Tree.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TransparencyLogInstance.public_key
documentation:
The public key used to verify signatures generated by the log. This
attribute contains the signature algorithm used by the log.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TransparencyLogInstance.log_id
documentation:
The unique identifier for this transparency log. Represented as the SHA-256
hash of the log's public key, calculated over the DER encoding of the key
represented as SubjectPublicKeyInfo. See https://www.rfc-
editor.org/rfc/rfc6962#section-3.2

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TransparencyLogInstance.checkpoint_key_id
documentation:
The checkpoint key identifier for the log used in a checkpoint. Optional,
not provided for logs that do not generate checkpoints. For logs that do
generate checkpoints, if not set, assume log_id equals checkpoint_key_id.
Follows the specification described here for ECDSA and Ed25519 signatures:
https://github.com/C2SP/C2SP/blob/main/signed-note.md#signatures For RSA
signatures, the key ID will match the ECDSA format, the hashed DER-encoded
SPKI public key. Publicly witnessed logs MUST NOT use RSA-signed
checkpoints, since witnesses do not support RSA signatures. This is
provided for convenience. Clients can also calculate the checkpoint key ID
given the log's public key. SHOULD be set for logs generating Ed25519
signatures. SHOULD be 4 bytes long, as a truncated hash.

TransparencyLogInstance describes the immutable parameters from a
transparency log. See https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9162.html#name-log-
parameters for more details. The included parameters are the minimal set
required to identify a log, and verify an inclusion proof/promise.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.CertificateAuthority

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.CertificateAuthority.subject
documentation:
The root certificate MUST be self-signed, and so the subject and issuer are
the same.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.CertificateAuthority.uri
documentation:
The URI identifies the certificate authority. It is RECOMMENDED that the
URI is the base URL for the certificate authority, that can be provided to
any SDK/client provided by the certificate authority to interact with the
certificate authority.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.CertificateAuthority.cert_chain
documentation:
The certificate chain for this CA. The last certificate in the chain MUST
be the trust anchor. The trust anchor MAY be a self-signed root CA
certificate or MAY be an intermediate CA certificate.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.CertificateAuthority.valid_for
documentation:
The time the *entire* chain was valid. This is at max the longest interval
when *all* certificates in the chain were valid, but it MAY be shorter.
Clients MUST check timestamps against *both* the `valid_for` time range
*and* the entire certificate chain. The TimeRange should be considered
valid *inclusive* of the endpoints.

CertificateAuthority enlists the information required to identify which CA
to use and perform signature verification.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TrustedRoot

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TrustedRoot.media_type
documentation:
MUST be application/vnd.dev.sigstore.trustedroot.v0.1+json when encoded as
JSON. Clients MUST be able to process and parse content with the media type
defined in the old format:
application/vnd.dev.sigstore.trustedroot+json;version=0.1

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TrustedRoot.tlogs
documentation: A set of trusted Rekor servers.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TrustedRoot.certificate_authorities
documentation:
A set of trusted certificate authorities (e.g Fulcio), and any intermediate
certificates they provide. If a CA is issuing multiple intermediate
certificate, each combination shall be represented as separate chain. I.e,
a single root cert may appear in multiple chains but with different
intermediate and/or leaf certificates. The certificates are intended to be
used for verifying artifact signatures.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TrustedRoot.ctlogs
documentation: A set of trusted certificate transparency logs.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.TrustedRoot.timestamp_authorities
documentation: A set of trusted timestamping authorities.

TrustedRoot describes the client's complete set of trusted entities. How
the TrustedRoot is populated is not specified, but can be a combination of
many sources such as TUF repositories, files on disk etc. The TrustedRoot
is not meant to be used for any artifact verification, only to capture the
complete/global set of trusted verification materials. When verifying an
artifact, based on the artifact and policies, a selection of
keys/authorities are expected to be extracted and provided to the
verification function. This way the set of keys/authorities can be kept to
a minimal set by the policy to gain better control over what signatures
that are allowed. The embedded transparency logs, CT logs, CAs and TSAs
MUST include any previously used instance -- otherwise signatures made in
the past cannot be verified. All the listed instances SHOULD be sorted by
the 'valid_for' in ascending order, that is, the oldest instance first.
Only the last instance is allowed to have their 'end' timestamp unset. All
previous instances MUST have a closed interval of validity. The last
instance MAY have a closed interval. Clients MUST accept instances that
overlaps in time, if not clients may experience problems during rotations
of verification materials. To be able to manage planned rotations of either
transparency logs or certificate authorities, clienst MUST accept lists of
instances where the last instance have a 'valid_for' that belongs to the
future. This should not be a problem as clients SHOULD first seek the trust
root for a suitable instance before creating a per artifact trust root
(that is, a sub-set of the complete trust root) that is used for
verification.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.SigningConfig

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.SigningConfig.ca_url
documentation:
A URL to a Fulcio-compatible CA, capable of receiving Certificate Signing
Requests (CSRs) and responding with issued certificates. This URL **MUST**
be the "base" URL for the CA, which clients should construct an appropriate
CSR endpoint on top of. For example, if `ca_url` is
`https://example.com/ca`, then the client **MAY** construct the CSR
endpoint as `https://example.com/ca/api/v2/signingCert`.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.SigningConfig.oidc_url
documentation:
A URL to an OpenID Connect identity provider. This URL **MUST** be the
"base" URL for the OIDC IdP, which clients should perform well-known OpenID
Connect discovery against.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.SigningConfig.tlog_urls
documentation:
One or more URLs to Rekor-compatible transparency log. Each URL **MUST** be
the "base" URL for the transparency log, which clients should construct
appropriate API endpoints on top of.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.SigningConfig.tsa_urls
documentation:
One ore more URLs to RFC 3161 Time Stamping Authority (TSA). Each URL
**MUST** be the **full** URL for the TSA, meaning that it should be
suitable for submitting Time Stamp Requests (TSRs) to via HTTP, per RFC
3161.

SigningConfig represents the trusted entities/state needed by Sigstore
signing. In particular, it primarily contains service URLs that a Sigstore
signer may need to connect to for the online aspects of signing.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.ClientTrustConfig

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.ClientTrustConfig.media_type
documentation: MUST be application/vnd.dev.sigstore.clienttrustconfig.v0.1+json

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.ClientTrustConfig.trusted_root
documentation: The root of trust, which MUST be present.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.trustroot.v1.ClientTrustConfig.signing_config
documentation: Configuration for signing clients, which MUST be present.

ClientTrustConfig describes the complete state needed by a client to
perform both signing and verification operations against a particular
instance of Sigstore.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.google.api.FieldBehavior

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.google.api.FieldBehavior.FIELD_BEHAVIOR_UNSPECIFIED
documentation: Conventional default for enums. Do not use this.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.google.api.FieldBehavior.OPTIONAL
documentation:
Specifically denotes a field as optional. While all fields in protocol
buffers are optional, this may be specified for emphasis if appropriate.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.google.api.FieldBehavior.REQUIRED
documentation:
Denotes a field as required. This indicates that the field **must** be
provided as part of the request, and failure to do so will cause an error
(usually `INVALID_ARGUMENT`).

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.google.api.FieldBehavior.OUTPUT_ONLY
documentation:
Denotes a field as output only. This indicates that the field is provided
in responses, but including the field in a request does nothing (the server
*must* ignore it and *must not* throw an error as a result of the field's
presence).

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.google.api.FieldBehavior.INPUT_ONLY
documentation:
Denotes a field as input only. This indicates that the field is provided in
requests, and the corresponding field is not included in output.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.google.api.FieldBehavior.IMMUTABLE
documentation:
Denotes a field as immutable. This indicates that the field may be set once
in a request to create a resource, but may not be changed thereafter.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.google.api.FieldBehavior.UNORDERED_LIST
documentation:
Denotes that a (repeated) field is an unordered list. This indicates that
the service may provide the elements of the list in any arbitrary order,
rather than the order the user originally provided. Additionally, the
list's order may or may not be stable.

An indicator of the behavior of a given field (for example, that a field is
required in requests, or given as output but ignored as input). This **does
not** change the behavior in protocol buffers itself; it only denotes the
behavior and may affect how API tooling handles the field. Note: This enum
**may** receive new values in the future.

modules.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1

This file has been @generated

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.HashAlgorithm
documentation:
Only a subset of the secure hash standard algorithms are supported. See
<https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/FIPS/NIST.FIPS.180-4.pdf> for more
details. UNSPECIFIED SHOULD not be used, primary reason for inclusion is to
force any proto JSON serialization to emit the used hash algorithm, as
default option is to *omit* the default value of an enum (which is the
first value, represented by '0'.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKeyDetails

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKeyDetails.PKCS1_RSA_PKCS1V5
documentation: RSA

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKeyDetails.PKIX_RSA_PKCS1V15_2048_SHA256
documentation: RSA public key in PKIX format, PKCS#1v1.5 signature

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKeyDetails.PKIX_RSA_PSS_2048_SHA256
documentation: RSA public key in PKIX format, RSASSA-PSS signature

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKeyDetails.PKIX_ECDSA_P256_HMAC_SHA_256
documentation: ECDSA

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKeyDetails.LMS_SHA256
documentation:
LMS and LM-OTS These keys and signatures may be used by private Sigstore
deployments, but are not currently supported by the public good instance.
USER WARNING: LMS and LM-OTS are both stateful signature schemes. Using
them correctly requires discretion and careful consideration to ensure that
individual secret keys are not used more than once. In addition, LM-OTS is
a single-use scheme, meaning that it MUST NOT be used for more than one
signature per LM-OTS key. If you cannot maintain these invariants, you MUST
NOT use these schemes.

Details of a specific public key, capturing the the key encoding method,
and signature algorithm. PublicKeyDetails captures the public key/hash
algorithm combinations recommended in the Sigstore ecosystem. This is
modelled as a linear set as we want to provide a small number of
opinionated options instead of allowing every possible permutation. Any
changes to this enum MUST be reflected in the algorithm registry. See:
docs/algorithm-registry.md To avoid the possibility of contradicting
formats such as PKCS1 with ED25519 the valid permutations are listed as a
linear set instead of a cartesian set (i.e one combined variable instead of
two, one for encoding and one for the signature algorithm).

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.SubjectAlternativeNameType.OTHER_NAME
documentation:
OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.57264.1.7 See
https://github.com/sigstore/fulcio/blob/main/docs/oid-info.md#1361415726417
--othername-san for more details.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.HashOutput

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.HashOutput.digest
documentation:
This is the raw octets of the message digest as computed by the hash
algorithm.

HashOutput captures a digest of a 'message' (generic octet sequence) and
the corresponding hash algorithm used.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.MessageSignature

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.MessageSignature.message_digest
documentation:
Message digest can be used to identify the artifact. Clients MUST NOT
attempt to use this digest to verify the associated signature; it is
intended solely for identification.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.MessageSignature.signature
documentation:
The raw bytes as returned from the signature algorithm. The signature
algorithm (and so the format of the signature bytes) are determined by the
contents of the 'verification_material', either a key-pair or a
certificate. If using a certificate, the certificate contains the required
information on the signature algorithm. When using a key pair, the
algorithm MUST be part of the public key, which MUST be communicated out-
of-band.
MessageSignature stores the computed signature over a message.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.LogId

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.LogId.key_id
documentation: The unique identity of the log, represented by its public key.
LogId captures the identity of a transparency log.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.Rfc3161SignedTimestamp

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.Rfc3161SignedTimestamp.signed_timestamp
documentation:
Signed timestamp is the DER encoded TimeStampResponse. See https://www.rfc-
editor.org/rfc/rfc3161.html#section-2.4.2
This message holds a RFC 3161 timestamp.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKey.raw_bytes
documentation:
DER-encoded public key, encoding method is specified by the key_details
attribute.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKey.key_details
documentation: Key encoding and signature algorithm to use for this key.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKey.valid_for
documentation: Optional validity period for this key, *inclusive* of the endpoints.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKeyIdentifier

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.PublicKeyIdentifier.hint
documentation:
Optional unauthenticated hint on which key to use. The format of the hint
must be agreed upon out of band by the signer and the verifiers, and so is
not subject to this specification. Example use-case is to specify the
public key to use, from a trusted key-ring. Implementors are RECOMMENDED to
derive the value from the public key as described in RFC 6962. See:
<https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6962#section-3.2>

PublicKeyIdentifier can be used to identify an (out of band) delivered key,
to verify a signature.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.ObjectIdentifier
documentation: An ASN.1 OBJECT IDENTIFIER

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.ObjectIdentifierValuePair
documentation: An OID and the corresponding (byte) value.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.X509Certificate.raw_bytes
documentation: DER-encoded X.509 certificate.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.SubjectAlternativeName.regexp
documentation: A regular expression describing the expected value for the SAN.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.SubjectAlternativeName.value
documentation: The exact value to match against.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.X509CertificateChain

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.X509CertificateChain.certificates
documentation:
One or more DER-encoded certificates. In some contexts (such as
`VerificationMaterial.x509_certificate_chain`), this sequence has an
imposed order. Unless explicitly specified, there is otherwise no
guaranteed order.

A collection of X.509 certificates. This "chain" can be used in multiple
contexts, such as providing a root CA certificate within a TUF root of
trust or multiple untrusted certificates for the purpose of chain building.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.common.v1.TimeRange
documentation:
The time range is closed and includes both the start and end times, (i.e.,
[start, end]). End is optional to be able to capture a period that has
started but has no known end.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.CertificateIdentity

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.CertificateIdentity.issuer
documentation: The X.509v3 issuer extension (OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.57264.1.1)

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.CertificateIdentity.oids
documentation:
An unordered list of OIDs that must be verified. All OID/values provided in
this list MUST exactly match against the values in the certificate for
verification to be successful.
The identity of a X.509 Certificate signer.

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptions

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptions.public_keys
documentation:
To simplify verification implementation, the logic for bundle verification
should be implemented as a higher-order function, where one of argument
should be an interface over the set of trusted public keys, like this:
`Verify(bytes artifact, bytes signature, string key_id)`. This way the
caller is in full control of mapping the identified (or hinted) key in the
bundle to one of the trusted keys, as this process is inherently
application specific.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptions.tlog_options
documentation:
Optional options for artifact transparency log verification. If none is
provided, the default verification options are: Threshold: 1 Online
verification: false Disable: false

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptions.ctlog_options
documentation:
Optional options for certificate transparency log verification. If none is
provided, the default verification options are: Threshold: 1 Disable: false

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptions.tsa_options
documentation:
Optional options for certificate signed timestamp verification. If none is
provided, the default verification options are: Threshold: 0 Disable: true

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptions.integrated_ts_options
documentation:
Optional options for integrated timestamp verification. If none is
provided, the default verification options are: Threshold: 0 Disable: true

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptions.observer_options
documentation:
Optional options for observed timestamp verification. If none is provided,
the default verification options are: Threshold 1 Disable: false

A light-weight set of options/policies for identifying trusted signers,
used during verification of a single artifact.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsTlogOptions.threshold
documentation: Number of transparency logs the entry must appear on.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsTlogOptions.perform_online_verification
documentation: Perform an online inclusion proof.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsTlogOptions.disable
documentation: Disable verification for transparency logs.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsCtlogOptions.threshold
documentation: The number of ct transparency logs the certificate must appear on.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsCtlogOptions.disable
documentation: Disable ct transparency log verification

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsTimestampAuthorityOptions.threshold
documentation: The number of signed timestamps that are expected.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsTimestampAuthorityOptions.disable
documentation: Disable signed timestamp verification.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsTlogIntegratedTimestampOptions.threshold
documentation: The number of integrated timestamps that are expected.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsTlogIntegratedTimestampOptions.disable
documentation: Disable integrated timestamp verification.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsObserverTimestampOptions.threshold
documentation:
The number of external observers of the timestamp. This is a union of
RFC3161 signed timestamps, and integrated timestamps from a transparency
log, that could include additional timestamp sources in the future.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.ArtifactVerificationOptionsObserverTimestampOptions.disable
documentation: Disable observer timestamp verification.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.Artifact.artifact_uri
documentation: Location of the artifact

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.Artifact.artifact
documentation: The raw bytes of the artifact

classes.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.Input

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.Input.artifact_trust_root
documentation:
The verification materials provided during a bundle verification. The
running process is usually preloaded with a "global"
dev.sisgtore.trustroot.TrustedRoot.v1 instance. Prior to verifying an
artifact (i.e a bundle), and/or based on current policy, some selection is
expected to happen, to filter out the exact certificate authority to use,
which transparency logs are relevant etc. The result should b ecaptured in
the `artifact_trust_root`.

class_variables.name: sigstore_protobuf_specs.dev.sigstore.verification.v1.Input.artifact
documentation: If the bundle contains a message signature, the artifact must be provided.

Input captures all that is needed to call the bundle verification method,
to verify a single artifact referenced by the bundle.
